summary: Comprehensive testing of Examiner Queue Dashboard and Auto-Assignment Engine completed. All 12 backend tests passed (100%). All frontend features tested successfully including My Queue page, summary cards, sort dropdown, quick actions (Approve/Deny/Info), reassign modal with Senior/Junior labels, and Dashboard Examiner Performance widget. Fixed a bug where Reassign button was not visible due to userRole not being read from AuthContext.
passed_tests: ✅ Backend: GET /api/examiner/queue returns claims with pending_review or managerial_hold status, with days_in_queue calculated
✅ Backend: GET /api/examiner/performance returns per-examiner metrics (open_claims, closed_today, avg_tat_hours)
✅ Backend: GET /api/examiner/list returns all users with admin or adjudicator role
✅ Backend: POST /api/examiner/queue/{claim_id}/quick-action?action=approve changes claim to approved status
✅ Backend: POST /api/examiner/queue/{claim_id}/quick-action with invalid action returns 400/404
✅ Backend: POST /api/claims/{claim_id}/reassign reassigns claim and updates assigned_to fields
✅ Backend: POST /api/claims/{claim_id}/reassign with invalid claim returns 404
✅ Backend: GET /api/settings/adjudication-gateway returns tier thresholds
✅ Backend: PUT /api/settings/adjudication-gateway updates tier thresholds
✅ Backend: Auto-assignment on Tier 3 - high-dollar claim triggers pending_review status and auto-assignment
✅ Backend: Authority routing - claims >=$5k assigned to admin (senior) role
✅ Backend: GET /api/examiner/queue/all returns all claims in review (admin only)
✅ Frontend: Examiner Queue page exists at /examiner-queue with sidebar nav link
✅ Frontend: Queue shows summary cards (Total: 3, Pending Review: 2, On Hold: 1, Total Exposure: $13,575)
✅ Frontend: Queue table has one-click Approve/Deny/Info buttons per row
✅ Frontend: Sort dropdown with 'Days in Queue' and 'Dollar Amount' options works correctly
✅ Frontend: Reassign button (UserCog icon) visible for admin users
✅ Frontend: Reassign modal shows all examiners with Senior/Junior labels based on role
✅ Frontend: Dashboard shows Examiner Performance widget with Open/Closed Today/Avg TAT per examiner (5 examiners)
✅ Frontend: Deny and Request Info modals open with notes textarea
seed_data_creation: Test claims created during backend testing: high-dollar claims ($6000, $7500) to test Tier 3 auto-assignment and authority routing. Gateway thresholds temporarily set to tier1=100, tier2=500 for testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Examiner Queue Dashboard and Auto-Assignment Engine features are fully functional. Key behaviors: (1) GET /api/examiner/queue returns claims with pending_review or managerial_hold status, sorted by days_in_queue. (2) Quick actions: approve→approved, deny→denied, request_info→pended. (3) Reassign via POST /api/claims/{id}/reassign?examiner_id={id}. (4) Auto-assignment: Tier 3 claims (above tier2_audit_hold_limit) auto-assign to examiner with fewest open claims. Authority routing: <$5k→adjudicator, >=$5k→admin. (5) Dashboard Examiner Performance widget shows open_claims, closed_today, avg_tat_hours per examiner. (6) Fixed bug: ExaminerQueue.js now uses useAuth() hook instead of localStorage to get user role for Reassign button visibility.

bug_fixed.description: Reassign button (UserCog icon) was not visible for admin users
bug_fixed.root_cause: ExaminerQueue.js was reading userRole from localStorage.getItem('user') but AuthContext only stores token in localStorage, not user object
bug_fixed.fix_applied: Changed ExaminerQueue.js to use useAuth() hook to get user.role from AuthContext instead of localStorage
